codeReview.summary: Solid project scaffolding that meets all requirements. All 21+ required dependencies are in package.json. TypeScript strict mode enabled. Tailwind CSS 4 correctly configured with CSS-first approach (@import 'tailwindcss' and @theme block with custom colors). PostCSS uses @tailwindcss/postcss. Directory structure matches architecture.md exactly — all 6 page routes, 9 API routes, component directories, db layer with query stubs, and lib utilities created. 13 tests pass covering format, utils, and categories. All verification steps confirmed: npm install, tsc --noEmit, next build, and vitest run all pass. One justified deviation: eslint.config.mjs (flat config) created instead of .eslintrc.json, since Next.js 16 removed `next lint` and defaults to ESLint flat config.

issues.description: The lint script uses `eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx` but ESLint 9+ with flat config does not support the --ext flag (deprecated). Should be simplified to `eslint .` to match services.yaml. May emit deprecation warnings.

Feature description specified creating `.eslintrc.json` but worker created `eslint.config.mjs` (flat config format). This is a justified deviation — Next.js 16 uses flat ESLint config and removed `next lint`. The research docs (.factory/research/nextjs-16.md) confirm this is the correct approach.

Test environment set to 'node' but @testing-library/react and jsdom are installed as devDependencies. Future component tests will need 'jsdom' environment. Acceptable for now since only lib tests exist — can be changed per-file or globally when component tests are added.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Feature description says to create `.eslintrc.json` but the correct approach for Next.js 16 is `eslint.config.mjs` (flat config). The feature description should be updated to match reality. AGENTS.md doesn't mention which ESLint config format to use.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/research/nextjs-16.md section 10 and 12: 'next lint command removed', '@next/eslint-plugin-next defaults to ESLint Flat Config format'. Worker correctly created eslint.config.mjs instead.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The setup-worker skill says to 'Write tests first (TDD)' and 'Run npx vitest run to confirm tests FAIL (red phase)'. For project scaffolding where lib files and tests are created together, TDD red-green is not naturally applicable. The worker's skillFeedback correctly noted this. The skill could clarify that TDD applies more to features with existing code/schema, not initial scaffolding.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The setup-worker skill mentions TDD (write failing tests first, red phase), but for project scaffolding the tests naturally pass immediately after creating the lib files since there's nothing to fail first.'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The lint script in package.json uses deprecated --ext flag (`eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx`) while services.yaml uses `npx eslint .`. These should be consistent — the --ext flag is deprecated in ESLint 9+ flat config.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: package.json line 12: `"lint": "eslint . --ext .ts,.tsx"` vs services.yaml: `lint: npx eslint .`

sharedStateObservations.observation: The setup-worker skill says to verify with agent-browser for any visible UI features. Worker skipped this for placeholder pages, which is reasonable since next build already compiles all pages. However, the skill could note that browser verification is optional for placeholder/stub pages.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill step 6 says 'If the feature creates the app shell or any visible UI, start the dev server and verify with agent-browser'. Worker did not perform browser verification but next build succeeded with all 18 routes.

summary: Project scaffolding passes review. All required dependencies, config files, and directory structure are correctly set up. TypeScript strict mode, Tailwind CSS 4 CSS-first config, and PostCSS are properly configured. 54 files created covering all app routes, API routes, components, db layer, lib utilities, and tests. All verification commands pass (npm install, tsc, build, vitest). Three non-blocking issues noted: deprecated --ext flag in lint script, eslint.config.mjs vs .eslintrc.json (justified deviation for Next.js 16), and vitest environment set to 'node' when jsdom is installed for future component tests.
